plain_english: Score rises when concrete, corroborated escalation events dominate.
Score falls only when stabilizing signals include verifiable operational follow-through.
Source reputation improves confidence, but cannot turn weak commentary into high impact.
Uncertainty is higher when signals conflict or corroboration is shallow.
model_mechanics: Pipeline: ingest -> normalize -> classify -> event-cluster -> bounded state update -> bounded probability.
Event clusters are grouped by actor pair, signal class, time bucket, and normalized title tokens.
Signal hierarchy suppresses rhetoric relative to operational events (action/movement/strategic).
De-escalation is discounted unless verification language is present (inspection/implemented/stand-down/rollback).
transparency: Per-driver source weight, corroboration count, and confidence are surfaced.
Nerdy Details documents hierarchy and de-escalation eligibility rules.
Back-history metadata reports replay mode and days covered.
Build metadata and update timestamp are shown on page.
limitations: Semantic clustering is token/time based and can still miss nuanced same-event links.
Archive/replay coverage quality varies by date and external API completeness.
Model confidence is not real-world truth confidence.
Backtesting is present but still lightweight and should be expanded.
example_scenarios: One missile test from one source -> medium event impact with higher uncertainty.
Same operational event corroborated by many outlets -> stronger confidence, bounded impact increase.
Many articles about one speech -> clustered and strongly down-weighted vs operational actions.
Ceasefire headline without verification -> weak stabilization contribution.
